2603.20854.title: SozKZ: Training Efficient Small Language Models for Kazakh from Scratch
2603.20854.paper_summary: Kazakh, a Turkic language spoken by over 22 million people, remains underserved by existing multilingual language models, which allocate minimal capacity to low-resource languages and employ tokenizers ill-suited to agglutinative morphology. We present SozKZ, a family of Llama-architecture language models (50M-600M parameters) trained entirely from scratch on 9 billion tokens of Kazakh text with a dedicated 50K BPE tokenizer. We evaluate all models on three Kazakh benchmarks -- multiple-choice cultural QA, reading comprehension (Belebele), and topic classification (SIB-200) -- alongside five multilingual baselines ranging from 500M to 3B parameters. Our 600M model achieves 30.3% accuracy on Kazakh cultural QA, approaching the 32.0% of Llama-3.2-1B (2x larger), and 25.5% on SIB-200 topic classification, surpassing all evaluated multilingual models up to 2B parameters. We observe consistent scaling from 50M to 600M, with MC QA accuracy rising from 22.8% to 30.3%, suggesting that further scaling remains beneficial. These results demonstrate that small, dedicated models trained from scratch with a language-appropriate tokenizer offer a viable path for low-resource language technology, achieving competitive performance at a fraction of the computational cost. All models and the tokenizer are released under open licenses.
2603.20854.paper_summary_zh: 哈薩克語是一種由超過2200萬人使用的突厥語言，現有的多語言語言模型對其支持不足，這些模型對低資源語言分配的能力有限，且使用的分詞器不適合聚合形態學。我們提出了SozKZ，一系列基於Llama架構的語言模型（50M-600M參數），這些模型完全從零開始訓練，使用了90億個哈薩克文本的標記，並配備了一個專用的50K BPE分詞器。我們在三個哈薩克基準上評估所有模型——多選文化問答、閱讀理解（Belebele）和主題分類（SIB-200），並與五個參數範圍從500M到3B的多語言基準進行比較。我們的600M模型在哈薩克文化問答中達到30.3%的準確率，接近Llama-3.2-1B（大2倍）的32.0%，在SIB-200主題分類中達到25.5%，超過了所有評估的多語言模型，這些模型的參數最高達到2B。我們觀察到從50M到600M的一致擴展，MC QA的準確率從22.8%上升到30.3%，這表明進一步擴展仍然是有益的。這些結果表明，使用適合語言的分詞器從零開始訓練的小型專用模型為低資源語言技術提供了一條可行的道路，以較低的計算成本實現競爭力的表現。所有模型和分詞器均以開放許可釋出。
2603.20854.author: Saken Tukenov et.al.
